61	class InputBatch:
62	
63	    def __init__(
64	        self,
65	        max_num_reqs: int,
66	        max_model_len: int,
67	        max_num_batched_tokens: int,
68	        device: torch.device,
69	        pin_memory: bool,
70	        vocab_size: int,
71	        block_sizes: list[int],  # The block_size of each kv cache group
72	        is_spec_decode: bool = False,
73	        logits_processing_needs_token_ids: bool = False,
74	    ):
75	        self.is_spec_decode = is_spec_decode
76	        self.max_num_reqs = max_num_reqs
77	        self.max_model_len = max_model_len
78	        self.max_num_batched_tokens = max_num_batched_tokens
79	        self.device = device
80	        self.pin_memory = pin_memory
81	        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
82	        self.logits_processing_needs_token_ids = (
83	            logits_processing_needs_token_ids)
84	
85	        self._req_ids: list[Optional[str]] = []
86	        self.req_id_to_index: dict[str, int] = {}
87	
88	        # TODO(woosuk): This buffer could be too large if max_model_len is big.
89	        # Find a way to reduce the CPU memory usage.
90	        # This buffer is not directly transferred to the GPU, so it does not
91	        # need to be pinned.
92	        self.token_ids_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros(
93	            (max_num_reqs, max_model_len),
94	            device="cpu",
95	            dtype=torch.int32,
96	            pin_memory=False,
97	        )
98	        self.token_ids_cpu = self.token_ids_cpu_tensor.numpy()
99	        self.num_tokens = np.zeros(max_num_reqs, dtype=np.int32)
100	        self.num_tokens_no_spec = np.zeros(max_num_reqs, dtype=np.int32)
101	        self.num_prompt_tokens = np.zeros(max_num_reqs, dtype=np.int32)
102	        self.num_computed_tokens_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros(
103	            (max_num_reqs, ),
104	            device="cpu",
105	            dtype=torch.int32,
106	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
107	        )
108	        self.num_computed_tokens_cpu = \
109	            self.num_computed_tokens_cpu_tensor.numpy()
110	
111	        # Block table.
112	        self.block_table = MultiGroupBlockTable(
113	            max_num_reqs=max_num_reqs,
114	            max_model_len=max_model_len,
115	            max_num_batched_tokens=max_num_batched_tokens,
116	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
117	            device=device,
118	            block_sizes=block_sizes,
119	        )
120	
121	        # Sampling-related.
122	        self.temperature = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
123	                                       dtype=torch.float32,
124	                                       device=device)
125	        self.temperature_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
126	                                                  dtype=torch.float32,
127	                                                  device="cpu",
128	                                                  pin_memory=pin_memory)
129	        self.temperature_cpu = self.temperature_cpu_tensor.numpy()
130	        self.greedy_reqs: set[str] = set()
131	        self.random_reqs: set[str] = set()
132	
133	        self.top_p = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
134	                                 dtype=torch.float32,
135	                                 device=device)
136	        self.top_p_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
137	                                            dtype=torch.float32,
138	                                            device="cpu",
139	                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
140	        self.top_p_cpu = self.top_p_cpu_tensor.numpy()
141	        self.top_p_reqs: set[str] = set()
142	
143	        self.top_k = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
144	                                 dtype=torch.int32,
145	                                 device=device)
146	        self.top_k_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
147	                                            dtype=torch.int32,
148	                                            device="cpu",
149	                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
150	        self.top_k_cpu = self.top_k_cpu_tensor.numpy()
151	        self.top_k_reqs: set[str] = set()
152	
153	        # IDs of requests which do not support spec decoding
154	        self.spec_decode_unsupported_reqs: set[str] = set()
155	
156	        # Frequency penalty related data structures
157	        self.frequency_penalties = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
158	                                               dtype=torch.float,
159	                                               device=device)
160	        self.frequency_penalties_cpu_tensor = torch.empty(
161	            (max_num_reqs, ),
162	            dtype=torch.float,
163	            device="cpu",
164	            pin_memory=pin_memory)
165	        self.frequency_penalties_cpu = \
166	            self.frequency_penalties_cpu_tensor.numpy()
167	        self.frequency_penalties_reqs: set[str] = set()
168	
169	        # Presence penalty related data structures
170	        self.presence_penalties = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
171	                                              dtype=torch.float,
172	                                              device=device)
173	        self.presence_penalties_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
174	                                                         dtype=torch.float,
175	                                                         device="cpu",
176	                                                         pin_memory=pin_memory)
177	        self.presence_penalties_cpu = self.presence_penalties_cpu_tensor.numpy(
178	        )
179	        self.presence_penalties_reqs: set[str] = set()
180	
181	        # Repetition penalty related data structures
182	        self.repetition_penalties = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
183	                                                dtype=torch.float,
184	                                                device=device)
185	        self.repetition_penalties_cpu_tensor = torch.empty(
186	            (max_num_reqs, ),
187	            dtype=torch.float,
188	            device="cpu",
189	            pin_memory=pin_memory)
190	        self.repetition_penalties_cpu = \
191	            self.repetition_penalties_cpu_tensor.numpy()
192	        self.repetition_penalties_reqs: set[str] = set()
193	
194	        # lora related
195	        self.request_lora_mapping = np.zeros((self.max_num_reqs, ),
196	                                             dtype=np.int32)
197	        self.lora_id_to_request_ids: dict[int, set[str]] = {}
198	        self.lora_id_to_lora_request: dict[int, LoRARequest] = {}
199	
200	        # req_index -> generator
201	        # NOTE(woosuk): The indices of the requests that do not have their own
202	        # generator should not be included in the dictionary.
203	        self.generators: dict[int, torch.Generator] = {}
204	
205	        self.num_logprobs: dict[str, int] = {}
206	        # NOTE(rob): num_prompt_logprobs only includes reqs
207	        # that are currently in the prefill phase.
208	        self.num_prompt_logprobs: dict[str, int] = {}
209	
210	        # To accumulate prompt logprobs tensor chunks across prefill steps.
211	        self.in_progress_prompt_logprobs_cpu: dict[str, LogprobsTensors] = {}
212	
213	        # Internal representation of per-step batch state changes, used for
214	        # reordering persistent batch and generating logitsprocs batch state
215	        # updates. Should reset each step.
216	        self.batch_update_builder = BatchUpdateBuilder()
217	
218	        # Define logits processors.
219	        # TODO(andy): logits processor list should be extensible via engine
220	        # constructor argument; for now the list is fixed.
221	        self.logitsprocs = init_builtin_logitsprocs(
222	            pin_memory_available=pin_memory,
223	            max_num_reqs=max_num_reqs + 1,
224	            device=device)
225	
226	        # TODO convert this to LogitsProcessor
227	        self.has_allowed_token_ids: set[str] = set()
228	        # NOTE(lufang): In the mask tensor, if the corresponding token allowed,
229	        # the value is False. Since we use masked_fill_ to set -inf.
230	        self.allowed_token_ids_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
231	        self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
232	
233	        # req_index -> bad_words_token_ids
234	        self.bad_words_token_ids: dict[int, list[list[int]]] = {}
235	
236	        self.req_output_token_ids: list[Optional[list[int]]] = []
237	
238	        # This is updated each time the batch constituents change.
239	        self.sampling_metadata = self._make_sampling_metadata()
240	
241	        self.pooling_params: dict[str, PoolingParams] = {}
242	
243	    @property
244	    def req_ids(self) -> list[str]:
245	        # None elements should only be present transiently
246	        # while performing state updates to the batch.
247	        return cast(list[str], self._req_ids)
248	
249	    def _get_next_add_index(self) -> int:
250	        if (req_index := self.batch_update_builder.pop_removed()) is not None:
251	            # Fill the empty index.
252	            return req_index
253	        # Append to end
254	        return self.num_reqs
255	
256	    def _register_add_request(self, request: "CachedRequestState") -> int:
257	        """Track add-request operations"""
258	        req_index = self._get_next_add_index()
259	        assert req_index < self.max_num_reqs
260	        params = (request.sampling_params
261	                  if request.sampling_params else request.pooling_params)
262	        self.batch_update_builder.added.append(
263	            (req_index, params, request.output_token_ids))
264	        return req_index
265	
266	    def add_request(
267	        self,
268	        request: "CachedRequestState",
269	    ) -> int:
270	        req_index = self._register_add_request(request)
271	
272	        req_id = request.req_id
273	        if req_index == len(self._req_ids):
274	            self._req_ids.append(req_id)
275	            self.req_output_token_ids.append(request.output_token_ids)
276	        else:
277	            self._req_ids[req_index] = req_id
278	            self.req_output_token_ids[req_index] = request.output_token_ids
279	
280	        self.req_id_to_index[req_id] = req_index
281	
282	        # Copy the prompt token ids and output token ids.
283	        num_prompt_tokens = len(request.prompt_token_ids)
284	        self.num_prompt_tokens[req_index] = num_prompt_tokens
285	        self.token_ids_cpu[
286	            req_index, :num_prompt_tokens] = request.prompt_token_ids
287	        start_idx = num_prompt_tokens
288	        end_idx = start_idx + len(request.output_token_ids)
289	        self.token_ids_cpu[req_index,
290	                           start_idx:end_idx] = request.output_token_ids
291	        # Number of token ids in token_ids_cpu.
292	        # NOTE(woosuk): This may include spec decode tokens.
293	        self.num_tokens[req_index] = request.num_tokens
294	        # Number of tokens without spec decode tokens.
295	        self.num_tokens_no_spec[req_index] = request.num_tokens
296	
297	        self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[req_index] = request.num_computed_tokens
298	        self.block_table.add_row(request.block_ids, req_index)
299	
300	        if sampling_params := request.sampling_params:
301	            if (self.is_spec_decode
302	                    and is_spec_decode_unsupported(sampling_params)):
303	                self.spec_decode_unsupported_reqs.add(req_id)
304	            if sampling_params.sampling_type == SamplingType.GREEDY:
305	                # Avoid later division by zero.
306	                self.temperature_cpu[req_index] = -1.0
307	                self.greedy_reqs.add(req_id)
308	            else:
309	                self.temperature_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.temperature
310	                self.random_reqs.add(req_id)
311	
312	            self.top_p_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.top_p
313	            if sampling_params.top_p < 1:
314	                self.top_p_reqs.add(req_id)
315	            top_k = sampling_params.top_k
316	            if 0 < top_k < self.vocab_size:
317	                self.top_k_reqs.add(req_id)
318	            else:
319	                top_k = self.vocab_size
320	            self.top_k_cpu[req_index] = top_k
321	            self.frequency_penalties_cpu[
322	                req_index] = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
323	            if sampling_params.frequency_penalty != 0.0:
324	                self.frequency_penalties_reqs.add(req_id)
325	            self.presence_penalties_cpu[
326	                req_index] = sampling_params.presence_penalty
327	            if sampling_params.presence_penalty != 0.0:
328	                self.presence_penalties_reqs.add(req_id)
329	            self.repetition_penalties_cpu[
330	                req_index] = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
331	            if sampling_params.repetition_penalty != 1.0:
332	                self.repetition_penalties_reqs.add(req_id)
333	
334	            # NOTE(woosuk): self.generators should not include the requests that
335	            # do not have their own generator.
336	            if request.generator is not None:
337	                self.generators[req_index] = request.generator
338	
339	            if sampling_params.logprobs is not None:
340	                self.num_logprobs[req_id] = sampling_params.logprobs
341	            if sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
342	                self.num_prompt_logprobs[
343	                    req_id] = sampling_params.prompt_logprobs
344	
345	            if sampling_params.allowed_token_ids:
346	                self.has_allowed_token_ids.add(req_id)
347	                if self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor is None:
348	                    # Lazy allocation for this tensor, which can be large.
349	                    # False means we don't fill with -inf.
350	                    self.allowed_token_ids_mask = torch.zeros(
351	                        self.max_num_reqs,
352	                        self.vocab_size,
353	                        dtype=torch.bool,
354	                        device=self.device)
355	                    self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros(
356	                        self.max_num_reqs,
357	                        self.vocab_size,
358	                        dtype=torch.bool,
359	                        device="cpu")
360	                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[req_index] = True
361	                # False means we don't fill with -inf.
362	                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[req_index][
363	                    sampling_params.allowed_token_ids] = False
364	
365	            if sampling_params.bad_words_token_ids:
366	                self.bad_words_token_ids[
367	                    req_index] = sampling_params.bad_words_token_ids
368	        else:
369	            assert request.pooling_params is not None
370	            self.pooling_params[req_id] = request.pooling_params
371	
372	        # Add request lora ID
373	        if request.lora_request:
374	            lora_id = request.lora_request.lora_int_id
375	            if lora_id not in self.lora_id_to_request_ids:
376	                self.lora_id_to_request_ids[lora_id] = set()
377	
378	            self.request_lora_mapping[req_index] = lora_id
379	            self.lora_id_to_request_ids[lora_id].add(request.req_id)
380	            self.lora_id_to_lora_request[lora_id] = request.lora_request
381	        else:
382	            # No LoRA
383	            self.request_lora_mapping[req_index] = 0
384	
385	        return req_index
386	
387	    def remove_request(self, req_id: str) -> Optional[int]:
388	        """This method must always be followed by a call to condense().
389	        
390	        Args:
391	          req_id: request to remove
392	
393	        Returns:
394	          Removed request index, or `None` if `req_id` not recognized
395	        """
396	
397	        req_index = self.req_id_to_index.pop(req_id, None)
398	        if req_index is None:
399	            return None
400	        self.batch_update_builder.removed_append(req_index)
401	        self._req_ids[req_index] = None
402	        self.req_output_token_ids[req_index] = None
403	
404	        self.greedy_reqs.discard(req_id)
405	        self.random_reqs.discard(req_id)
406	        self.top_p_reqs.discard(req_id)
407	        self.top_k_reqs.discard(req_id)
408	        self.spec_decode_unsupported_reqs.discard(req_id)
409	        self.frequency_penalties_reqs.discard(req_id)
410	        self.presence_penalties_reqs.discard(req_id)
411	        self.repetition_penalties_reqs.discard(req_id)
412	        self.generators.pop(req_index, None)
413	        self.num_logprobs.pop(req_id, None)
414	        self.num_prompt_logprobs.pop(req_id, None)
415	        self.in_progress_prompt_logprobs_cpu.pop(req_id, None)
416	
417	        # LoRA
418	        lora_id = self.request_lora_mapping[req_index]
419	        if lora_id != 0:
420	            self.lora_id_to_request_ids[lora_id].discard(req_id)
421	            if len(self.lora_id_to_request_ids[lora_id]) == 0:
422	                self.lora_id_to_request_ids.pop(lora_id)
423	                self.lora_id_to_lora_request.pop(lora_id)
424	            self.request_lora_mapping[req_index] = 0
425	
426	        self.has_allowed_token_ids.discard(req_id)
427	        if self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor is not None:
428	            # False means we don't fill with -inf.
429	            self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[req_index].fill_(False)
430	        self.bad_words_token_ids.pop(req_index, None)
431	        self.pooling_params.pop(req_id, None)
432	        return req_index
433	
434	    def swap_states(self, i1: int, i2: int) -> None:
435	        self.batch_update_builder.moved.append(
436	            (i1, i2, MoveDirectionality.SWAP))
437	        old_id_i1 = self._req_ids[i1]
438	        old_id_i2 = self._req_ids[i2]
439	        self._req_ids[i1], self._req_ids[i2] =\
440	            self._req_ids[i2], self._req_ids[i1] # noqa
441	        self.req_output_token_ids[i1], self.req_output_token_ids[i2] =\
442	            self.req_output_token_ids[i2], self.req_output_token_ids[i1]
443	        assert old_id_i1 is not None and old_id_i2 is not None
444	        self.req_id_to_index[old_id_i1], self.req_id_to_index[old_id_i2] =\
445	            self.req_id_to_index[old_id_i2], self.req_id_to_index[old_id_i1]
446	        self.num_tokens[i1], self.num_tokens[i2] =\
447	            self.num_tokens[i2], self.num_tokens[i1]
448	        self.num_tokens_no_spec[i1], self.num_tokens_no_spec[i2] =\
449	            self.num_tokens_no_spec[i2], self.num_tokens_no_spec[i1]
450	        self.num_prompt_tokens[i1], self.num_prompt_tokens[i2] =\
451	            self.num_prompt_tokens[i2], self.num_prompt_tokens[i1]
452	        self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[i1], self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[i2] =\
453	            self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[i2], self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[i1]
454	        self.temperature_cpu[i1], self.temperature_cpu[i2] =\
455	            self.temperature_cpu[i2], self.temperature_cpu[i1]
456	        self.top_p_cpu[i1], self.top_p_cpu[i2] =\
457	            self.top_p_cpu[i2], self.top_p_cpu[i1]
458	        self.top_k_cpu[i1], self.top_k_cpu[i2] =\
459	            self.top_k_cpu[i2], self.top_k_cpu[i1]
460	        self.frequency_penalties_cpu[i1], self.frequency_penalties_cpu[i2] =\
461	            self.frequency_penalties_cpu[i2], self.frequency_penalties_cpu[i1]
462	        self.presence_penalties_cpu[i1], self.presence_penalties_cpu[i2] =\
463	            self.presence_penalties_cpu[i2], self.presence_penalties_cpu[i1]
464	        self.repetition_penalties_cpu[i1], self.repetition_penalties_cpu[i2] =\
465	            self.repetition_penalties_cpu[i2], self.repetition_penalties_cpu[i1]
466	
467	        # NOTE: the following is unsafe
468	        # self.token_ids_cpu[i1, ...], self.token_ids_cpu[i2, ...], =\
469	        #     self.token_ids_cpu[i2, ...], self.token_ids_cpu[i1, ...]
470	        # instead, we need to temporiarily copy the data for one of the indices
471	        # TODO(lucas): optimize this by only copying valid indices
472	        tmp = self.token_ids_cpu[i1, ...].copy()
473	        self.token_ids_cpu[i1, ...] = self.token_ids_cpu[i2, ...]
474	        self.token_ids_cpu[i2, ...] = tmp
475	
476	        swap_dict_values(self.generators, i1, i2)
477	        swap_dict_values(self.bad_words_token_ids, i1, i2)
478	
479	        self.request_lora_mapping[i1], self.request_lora_mapping[i2] =\
480	            self.request_lora_mapping[i2], self.request_lora_mapping[i1]
481	
482	        if self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor is not None:
483	            self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[i1], \
484	                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[i2] =\
485	                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[i2], \
486	                    self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[i1]
487	        self.block_table.swap_row(i1, i2)
488	
489	    def condense(self) -> None:
490	        """Slide non-empty requests down into lower, empty indices.
491	
492	        Any consecutive empty indices at the very end of the list are not
493	        filled.
494	
495	        Args:
496	          empty_req_indices: empty indices which may be filled.
497	
498	        Returns:
499	          swaps: list of (from,to) swap tuples for moved requests
500	          empty_req_indices: indices not filled by condensation
501	        """
502	        if not (empty_req_indices := self.batch_update_builder.removed):
503	            # All removed requests were replaced by added requests, or else no
504	            # requests were removed at all. No condense() needed
505	            return
506	        num_reqs = self.num_reqs
507	        if num_reqs == 0:
508	            # The batched states are empty.
509	            self._req_ids.clear()
510	            self.req_output_token_ids.clear()
511	            return
512	
513	        # NOTE(woosuk): This function assumes that the empty_req_indices
514	        # is sorted in descending order.
515	        last_req_index = num_reqs + len(empty_req_indices) - 1
516	        while empty_req_indices:
517	            # Find the largest non-empty index.
518	            while last_req_index in empty_req_indices:
519	                last_req_index -= 1
520	
521	            # Find the smallest empty index.
522	            empty_index = self.batch_update_builder.peek_removed()
523	            assert empty_index is not None
524	            if empty_index >= last_req_index:
525	                break
526	
527	            # Move active request down into empty request
528	            # index.
529	            self.batch_update_builder.pop_removed()
530	            self.batch_update_builder.moved.append(
531	                (last_req_index, empty_index,
532	                 MoveDirectionality.UNIDIRECTIONAL))
533	            req_id = self._req_ids[last_req_index]
534	            output_token_ids = self.req_output_token_ids[last_req_index]
535	            assert req_id is not None
536	            self._req_ids[empty_index] = req_id
537	            self._req_ids[last_req_index] = None
538	            self.req_output_token_ids[empty_index] = output_token_ids
539	            self.req_output_token_ids[last_req_index] = None
540	            self.req_id_to_index[req_id] = empty_index
541	
542	            num_tokens = self.num_tokens[last_req_index]
543	            self.token_ids_cpu[empty_index, :num_tokens] = self.token_ids_cpu[
544	                last_req_index, :num_tokens]
545	            self.num_tokens[empty_index] = num_tokens
546	            self.num_tokens_no_spec[empty_index] = self.num_tokens_no_spec[
547	                last_req_index]
548	            self.num_prompt_tokens[empty_index] = self.num_prompt_tokens[
549	                last_req_index]
550	            self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[
551	                empty_index] = self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[last_req_index]
552	            self.block_table.move_row(last_req_index, empty_index)
553	            self.temperature_cpu[empty_index] = self.temperature_cpu[
554	                last_req_index]
555	            self.top_p_cpu[empty_index] = self.top_p_cpu[last_req_index]
556	            self.top_k_cpu[empty_index] = self.top_k_cpu[last_req_index]
557	            self.frequency_penalties_cpu[
558	                empty_index] = self.frequency_penalties_cpu[last_req_index]
559	            self.presence_penalties_cpu[
560	                empty_index] = self.presence_penalties_cpu[last_req_index]
561	            self.repetition_penalties_cpu[
562	                empty_index] = self.repetition_penalties_cpu[last_req_index]
563	            generator = self.generators.pop(last_req_index, None)
564	            if generator is not None:
565	                self.generators[empty_index] = generator
566	
567	            self.request_lora_mapping[empty_index] = self.request_lora_mapping[
568	                last_req_index]
569	
570	            # TODO convert these to LogitsProcessors
571	            if self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor is not None:
572	                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[
573	                    empty_index] = self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[
574	                        last_req_index]
575	
576	            bad_words_token_ids = self.bad_words_token_ids.pop(
577	                last_req_index, None)
578	            if bad_words_token_ids is not None:
579	                self.bad_words_token_ids[empty_index] = bad_words_token_ids
580	
581	            # Decrement last_req_index since it is now empty.
582	            last_req_index -= 1
583	
584	        # Trim lists to the batch size.
585	        del self._req_ids[self.num_reqs:]
586	        del self.req_output_token_ids[self.num_reqs:]
587	
588	    def refresh_metadata(self):
589	        """Apply batch updates, reset input batch at end of step
590	        
591	        * Apply batch add/remove/permute to logits procs' states
592	        * If batch state is modified, update sampling metadata
593	        """
594	        batch_update = self.batch_update_builder.get_and_reset(self.num_reqs)
595	        for logit_proc in self.logitsprocs.all:
596	            logit_proc.update_state(batch_update)
597	        if batch_update:
598	            self.sampling_metadata = self._make_sampling_metadata()
599	
600	    def _make_sampling_metadata(self) -> SamplingMetadata:
601	        num_reqs = self.num_reqs
602	        if not self.all_greedy:
603	            temperature = copy_slice(self.temperature_cpu_tensor,
604	                                     self.temperature, num_reqs)
605	        else:
606	            temperature = None
607	        if not self.no_top_p:
608	            copy_slice(self.top_p_cpu_tensor, self.top_p, num_reqs)
609	        if not self.no_top_k:
610	            copy_slice(self.top_k_cpu_tensor, self.top_k, num_reqs)
611	
612	        if not self.no_penalties:
613	            # Since syncing these tensors is expensive only copy them
614	            # if necessary i.e. if there are requests which require
615	            # penalties to be applied during sampling.
616	            copy_slice(self.frequency_penalties_cpu_tensor,
617	                       self.frequency_penalties, num_reqs)
618	            copy_slice(self.presence_penalties_cpu_tensor,
619	                       self.presence_penalties, num_reqs)
620	            copy_slice(self.repetition_penalties_cpu_tensor,
621	                       self.repetition_penalties, num_reqs)
622	
623	        needs_prompt_token_ids = (not self.no_penalties or
624	                                  (self.num_reqs > 0
625	                                   and self.logits_processing_needs_token_ids))
626	        if needs_prompt_token_ids:
627	            # The prompt tokens are used only for applying penalties or
628	            # step pooling during the sampling/pooling process.
629	            # Hence copy these tensors only when there are requests which
630	            # need penalties/step_pooler to be applied.
631	            prompt_token_ids = self._make_prompt_token_ids_tensor()
632	        else:
633	            prompt_token_ids = None
634	
635	        allowed_token_ids_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
636	        if not self.no_allowed_token_ids:
637	            assert self.allowed_token_ids_mask is not None
638	            copy_slice(self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor,
639	                       self.allowed_token_ids_mask, num_reqs)
640	            allowed_token_ids_mask = self.allowed_token_ids_mask[:num_reqs]
641	
642	        return SamplingMetadata(
643	            temperature=temperature,
644	            all_greedy=self.all_greedy,
645	            all_random=self.all_random,
646	            top_p=None if self.no_top_p else self.top_p[:num_reqs],
647	            top_k=None if self.no_top_k else self.top_k[:num_reqs],
648	            generators=self.generators,
649	            max_num_logprobs=self.max_num_logprobs,
650	            prompt_token_ids=prompt_token_ids,
651	            frequency_penalties=self.frequency_penalties[:num_reqs],
652	            presence_penalties=self.presence_penalties[:num_reqs],
653	            repetition_penalties=self.repetition_penalties[:num_reqs],
654	            output_token_ids=cast(list[list[int]], self.req_output_token_ids),
655	            no_penalties=self.no_penalties,
656	            allowed_token_ids_mask=allowed_token_ids_mask,
657	            bad_words_token_ids=self.bad_words_token_ids,
658	            logitsprocs=self.logitsprocs,
659	        )
660	
661	    @property
662	    def pooling_metadata(self) -> PoolingMetadata:
663	        if len(self.pooling_params) == 0:
664	            pooling_params = []
665	        else:
666	            # Note, for now this assumes that all request in the batch
667	            # are either sampling or pooling requests
668	            assert len(self.req_ids) == len(self.pooling_params)
669	            pooling_params = [
670	                self.pooling_params[req_id] for req_id in self.req_ids
671	            ]
672	
673	        return PoolingMetadata(
674	            prompt_lens=torch.from_numpy(
675	                self.num_prompt_tokens[:self.num_reqs]).to(self.device),
676	            prompt_token_ids=self.sampling_metadata.prompt_token_ids,
677	            pooling_params=pooling_params,
678	        )
679	
680	    def _make_prompt_token_ids_tensor(self) -> torch.Tensor:
681	        max_prompt_len = self.num_prompt_tokens[:self.num_reqs].max()
682	        prompt_token_ids_cpu_tensor = torch.empty(
683	            (self.num_reqs, max_prompt_len),
684	            device="cpu",
685	            dtype=torch.int64,
686	            pin_memory=self.pin_memory,
687	        )
688	        prompt_token_ids = prompt_token_ids_cpu_tensor.numpy()
689	        prompt_token_ids[:] = self.token_ids_cpu[:self.
690	                                                 num_reqs, :max_prompt_len]
691	        # Use the value of vocab_size as a pad since we don't have a
692	        # token_id of this value.
693	        for i in range(self.num_reqs):
694	            prompt_token_ids[i, self.num_prompt_tokens[i]:] = self.vocab_size
695	        return prompt_token_ids_cpu_tensor.to(device=self.device,
696	                                              non_blocking=True)
697	
698	    def make_lora_inputs(
699	        self, num_scheduled_tokens: np.ndarray
700	    ) -> tuple[tuple[int, ...], tuple[int, ...], set[LoRARequest]]:
701	        """
702	        Given the num_scheduled_tokens for each request in the batch, return
703	        datastructures used to activate the current LoRAs.
704	        Returns:
705	            1. prompt_lora_mapping: A tuple of size self.num_reqs where,
706	               prompt_lora_mapping[i] is the LoRA id to use for the ith prompt.
707	            2. token_lora_mapping: A tuple of size np.sum(num_scheduled_tokens)
708	               where, token_lora_mapping[i] is the LoRA id to use for ith token.
709	            3. lora_requests: Set of relevant LoRA requests.
710	        """
711	
712	        req_lora_mapping = self.request_lora_mapping[:self.num_reqs]
713	        prompt_lora_mapping = tuple(req_lora_mapping)
714	        token_lora_mapping = tuple(
715	            req_lora_mapping.repeat(num_scheduled_tokens))
716	        active_lora_requests: set[LoRARequest] = set(
717	            self.lora_id_to_lora_request.values())
718	
719	        return prompt_lora_mapping, token_lora_mapping, active_lora_requests
720	
721	    @property
722	    def num_reqs(self) -> int:
723	        return len(self.req_id_to_index)
724	
725	    @property
726	    def all_greedy(self) -> bool:
727	        return len(self.random_reqs) == 0
728	
729	    @property
730	    def all_random(self) -> bool:
731	        return len(self.greedy_reqs) == 0
732	
733	    @property
734	    def no_top_p(self) -> bool:
735	        return len(self.top_p_reqs) == 0
736	
737	    @property
738	    def no_top_k(self) -> bool:
739	        return len(self.top_k_reqs) == 0
740	
741	    @property
742	    def no_penalties(self) -> bool:
743	        return (len(self.presence_penalties_reqs) == 0
744	                and len(self.frequency_penalties_reqs) == 0
745	                and len(self.repetition_penalties_reqs) == 0)
746	
747	    @property
748	    def max_num_logprobs(self) -> Optional[int]:
749	        return max(self.num_logprobs.values()) if self.num_logprobs else None
750	
751	    @property
752	    def no_prompt_logprob(self) -> bool:
753	        return not self.num_prompt_logprobs
754	
755	    @property
756	    def no_allowed_token_ids(self) -> bool:
757	        return len(self.has_allowed_token_ids) == 0
758

# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright contributors to the vLLM project
# Datastructures defining a GPU input batch

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Optional, cast

import numpy as np
import torch

from vllm.lora.request import LoRARequest
from vllm.multimodal.inputs import MultiModalKwargs, PlaceholderRange
from vllm.pooling_params import PoolingParams
from vllm.sampling_params import SamplingParams, SamplingType
from vllm.utils import swap_dict_values
from vllm.v1.outputs import LogprobsTensors
from vllm.v1.pool.metadata import PoolingMetadata
from vllm.v1.sample.logits_processor import (BatchUpdateBuilder,
                                             MoveDirectionality,
                                             init_builtin_logitsprocs)
from vllm.v1.sample.metadata import SamplingMetadata
from vllm.v1.spec_decode.utils import is_spec_decode_unsupported
from vllm.v1.utils import copy_slice
from vllm.v1.worker.block_table import MultiGroupBlockTable


@dataclass
class CachedRequestState:

    req_id: str
    prompt_token_ids: list[int]
    mm_inputs: list[MultiModalKwargs]
    mm_positions: list[PlaceholderRange]
    sampling_params: Optional[SamplingParams]
    pooling_params: Optional[PoolingParams]
    generator: Optional[torch.Generator]

    block_ids: tuple[list[int], ...]
    num_computed_tokens: int
    output_token_ids: list[int]

    mrope_positions: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
    mrope_position_delta: Optional[int] = None

    lora_request: Optional[LoRARequest] = None

    def __post_init__(self):
        self.num_prompt_tokens = len(self.prompt_token_ids)

    @property
    def num_tokens(self) -> int:
        return self.num_prompt_tokens + len(self.output_token_ids)

    def get_token_id(self, idx: int) -> int:
        if idx < self.num_prompt_tokens:
            return self.prompt_token_ids[idx]
        else:
            return self.output_token_ids[idx - self.num_prompt_tokens]


class InputBatch:

    def __init__(
        self,
        max_num_reqs: int,
        max_model_len: int,
        max_num_batched_tokens: int,
        device: torch.device,
        pin_memory: bool,
        vocab_size: int,
        block_sizes: list[int],  # The block_size of each kv cache group
        is_spec_decode: bool = False,
        logits_processing_needs_token_ids: bool = False,
    ):
        self.is_spec_decode = is_spec_decode
        self.max_num_reqs = max_num_reqs
        self.max_model_len = max_model_len
        self.max_num_batched_tokens = max_num_batched_tokens
        self.device = device
        self.pin_memory = pin_memory
        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
        self.logits_processing_needs_token_ids = (
            logits_processing_needs_token_ids)

        self._req_ids: list[Optional[str]] = []
        self.req_id_to_index: dict[str, int] = {}

        # TODO(woosuk): This buffer could be too large if max_model_len is big.
        # Find a way to reduce the CPU memory usage.
        # This buffer is not directly transferred to the GPU, so it does not
        # need to be pinned.
        self.token_ids_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros(
            (max_num_reqs, max_model_len),
            device="cpu",
            dtype=torch.int32,
            pin_memory=False,
        )
        self.token_ids_cpu = self.token_ids_cpu_tensor.numpy()
        self.num_tokens = np.zeros(max_num_reqs, dtype=np.int32)
        self.num_tokens_no_spec = np.zeros(max_num_reqs, dtype=np.int32)
        self.num_prompt_tokens = np.zeros(max_num_reqs, dtype=np.int32)
        self.num_computed_tokens_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros(
            (max_num_reqs, ),
            device="cpu",
            dtype=torch.int32,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        self.num_computed_tokens_cpu = \
            self.num_computed_tokens_cpu_tensor.numpy()

        # Block table.
        self.block_table = MultiGroupBlockTable(
            max_num_reqs=max_num_reqs,
            max_model_len=max_model_len,
            max_num_batched_tokens=max_num_batched_tokens,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
            device=device,
            block_sizes=block_sizes,
        )

        # Sampling-related.
        self.temperature = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
                                       dtype=torch.float32,
                                       device=device)
        self.temperature_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
                                                  dtype=torch.float32,
                                                  device="cpu",
                                                  pin_memory=pin_memory)
        self.temperature_cpu = self.temperature_cpu_tensor.numpy()
        self.greedy_reqs: set[str] = set()
        self.random_reqs: set[str] = set()

        self.top_p = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
                                 dtype=torch.float32,
                                 device=device)
        self.top_p_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
                                            dtype=torch.float32,
                                            device="cpu",
                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
        self.top_p_cpu = self.top_p_cpu_tensor.numpy()
        self.top_p_reqs: set[str] = set()

        self.top_k = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
                                 dtype=torch.int32,
                                 device=device)
        self.top_k_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
                                            dtype=torch.int32,
                                            device="cpu",
                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
        self.top_k_cpu = self.top_k_cpu_tensor.numpy()
        self.top_k_reqs: set[str] = set()

        # IDs of requests which do not support spec decoding
        self.spec_decode_unsupported_reqs: set[str] = set()

        # Frequency penalty related data structures
        self.frequency_penalties = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
                                               dtype=torch.float,
                                               device=device)
        self.frequency_penalties_cpu_tensor = torch.empty(
            (max_num_reqs, ),
            dtype=torch.float,
            device="cpu",
            pin_memory=pin_memory)
        self.frequency_penalties_cpu = \
            self.frequency_penalties_cpu_tensor.numpy()
        self.frequency_penalties_reqs: set[str] = set()

        # Presence penalty related data structures
        self.presence_penalties = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
                                              dtype=torch.float,
                                              device=device)
        self.presence_penalties_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
                                                         dtype=torch.float,
                                                         device="cpu",
                                                         pin_memory=pin_memory)
        self.presence_penalties_cpu = self.presence_penalties_cpu_tensor.numpy(
        )
        self.presence_penalties_reqs: set[str] = set()

        # Repetition penalty related data structures
        self.repetition_penalties = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
                                                dtype=torch.float,
                                                device=device)
        self.repetition_penalties_cpu_tensor = torch.empty(
            (max_num_reqs, ),
            dtype=torch.float,
            device="cpu",
            pin_memory=pin_memory)
        self.repetition_penalties_cpu = \
            self.repetition_penalties_cpu_tensor.numpy()
        self.repetition_penalties_reqs: set[str] = set()

        # lora related
        self.request_lora_mapping = np.zeros((self.max_num_reqs, ),
                                             dtype=np.int32)
        self.lora_id_to_request_ids: dict[int, set[str]] = {}
        self.lora_id_to_lora_request: dict[int, LoRARequest] = {}

        # req_index -> generator
        # NOTE(woosuk): The indices of the requests that do not have their own
        # generator should not be included in the dictionary.
        self.generators: dict[int, torch.Generator] = {}

        self.num_logprobs: dict[str, int] = {}
        # NOTE(rob): num_prompt_logprobs only includes reqs
        # that are currently in the prefill phase.
        self.num_prompt_logprobs: dict[str, int] = {}

        # To accumulate prompt logprobs tensor chunks across prefill steps.
        self.in_progress_prompt_logprobs_cpu: dict[str, LogprobsTensors] = {}

        # Internal representation of per-step batch state changes, used for
        # reordering persistent batch and generating logitsprocs batch state
        # updates. Should reset each step.
        self.batch_update_builder = BatchUpdateBuilder()

        # Define logits processors.
        # TODO(andy): logits processor list should be extensible via engine
        # constructor argument; for now the list is fixed.
        self.logitsprocs = init_builtin_logitsprocs(
            pin_memory_available=pin_memory,
            max_num_reqs=max_num_reqs + 1,
            device=device)

        # TODO convert this to LogitsProcessor
        self.has_allowed_token_ids: set[str] = set()
        # NOTE(lufang): In the mask tensor, if the corresponding token allowed,
        # the value is False. Since we use masked_fill_ to set -inf.
        self.allowed_token_ids_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
        self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None

        # req_index -> bad_words_token_ids
        self.bad_words_token_ids: dict[int, list[list[int]]] = {}

        self.req_output_token_ids: list[Optional[list[int]]] = []

        # This is updated each time the batch constituents change.
        self.sampling_metadata = self._make_sampling_metadata()

        self.pooling_params: dict[str, PoolingParams] = {}

    @property
    def req_ids(self) -> list[str]:
        # None elements should only be present transiently
        # while performing state updates to the batch.
        return cast(list[str], self._req_ids)

    def _get_next_add_index(self) -> int:
        if (req_index := self.batch_update_builder.pop_removed()) is not None:
            # Fill the empty index.
            return req_index
        # Append to end
        return self.num_reqs

    def _register_add_request(self, request: "CachedRequestState") -> int:
        """Track add-request operations"""
        req_index = self._get_next_add_index()
        assert req_index < self.max_num_reqs
        params = (request.sampling_params
                  if request.sampling_params else request.pooling_params)
        self.batch_update_builder.added.append(
            (req_index, params, request.output_token_ids))
        return req_index

    def add_request(
        self,
        request: "CachedRequestState",
    ) -> int:
        req_index = self._register_add_request(request)

        req_id = request.req_id
        if req_index == len(self._req_ids):
            self._req_ids.append(req_id)
            self.req_output_token_ids.append(request.output_token_ids)
        else:
            self._req_ids[req_index] = req_id
            self.req_output_token_ids[req_index] = request.output_token_ids

        self.req_id_to_index[req_id] = req_index

        # Copy the prompt token ids and output token ids.
        num_prompt_tokens = len(request.prompt_token_ids)
        self.num_prompt_tokens[req_index] = num_prompt_tokens
        self.token_ids_cpu[
            req_index, :num_prompt_tokens] = request.prompt_token_ids
        start_idx = num_prompt_tokens
        end_idx = start_idx + len(request.output_token_ids)
        self.token_ids_cpu[req_index,
                           start_idx:end_idx] = request.output_token_ids
        # Number of token ids in token_ids_cpu.
        # NOTE(woosuk): This may include spec decode tokens.
        self.num_tokens[req_index] = request.num_tokens
        # Number of tokens without spec decode tokens.
        self.num_tokens_no_spec[req_index] = request.num_tokens

        self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[req_index] = request.num_computed_tokens
        self.block_table.add_row(request.block_ids, req_index)

        if sampling_params := request.sampling_params:
            if (self.is_spec_decode
                    and is_spec_decode_unsupported(sampling_params)):
                self.spec_decode_unsupported_reqs.add(req_id)
            if sampling_params.sampling_type == SamplingType.GREEDY:
                # Avoid later division by zero.
                self.temperature_cpu[req_index] = -1.0
                self.greedy_reqs.add(req_id)
            else:
                self.temperature_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.temperature
                self.random_reqs.add(req_id)

            self.top_p_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.top_p
            if sampling_params.top_p < 1:
                self.top_p_reqs.add(req_id)
            top_k = sampling_params.top_k
            if 0 < top_k < self.vocab_size:
                self.top_k_reqs.add(req_id)
            else:
                top_k = self.vocab_size
            self.top_k_cpu[req_index] = top_k
            self.frequency_penalties_cpu[
                req_index] = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
            if sampling_params.frequency_penalty != 0.0:
                self.frequency_penalties_reqs.add(req_id)
            self.presence_penalties_cpu[
                req_index] = sampling_params.presence_penalty
            if sampling_params.presence_penalty != 0.0:
                self.presence_penalties_reqs.add(req_id)
            self.repetition_penalties_cpu[
                req_index] = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
            if sampling_params.repetition_penalty != 1.0:
                self.repetition_penalties_reqs.add(req_id)

            # NOTE(woosuk): self.generators should not include the requests that
            # do not have their own generator.
            if request.generator is not None:
                self.generators[req_index] = request.generator

            if sampling_params.logprobs is not None:
                self.num_logprobs[req_id] = sampling_params.logprobs
            if sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
                self.num_prompt_logprobs[
                    req_id] = sampling_params.prompt_logprobs

            if sampling_params.allowed_token_ids:
                self.has_allowed_token_ids.add(req_id)
                if self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor is None:
                    # Lazy allocation for this tensor, which can be large.
                    # False means we don't fill with -inf.
                    self.allowed_token_ids_mask = torch.zeros(
                        self.max_num_reqs,
                        self.vocab_size,
                        dtype=torch.bool,
                        device=self.device)
                    self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros(
                        self.max_num_reqs,
                        self.vocab_size,
                        dtype=torch.bool,
                        device="cpu")
                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[req_index] = True
                # False means we don't fill with -inf.
                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[req_index][
                    sampling_params.allowed_token_ids] = False

            if sampling_params.bad_words_token_ids:
                self.bad_words_token_ids[
                    req_index] = sampling_params.bad_words_token_ids
        else:
            assert request.pooling_params is not None
            self.pooling_params[req_id] = request.pooling_params

        # Add request lora ID
        if request.lora_request:
            lora_id = request.lora_request.lora_int_id
            if lora_id not in self.lora_id_to_request_ids:
                self.lora_id_to_request_ids[lora_id] = set()

            self.request_lora_mapping[req_index] = lora_id
            self.lora_id_to_request_ids[lora_id].add(request.req_id)
            self.lora_id_to_lora_request[lora_id] = request.lora_request
        else:
            # No LoRA
            self.request_lora_mapping[req_index] = 0

        return req_index

    def remove_request(self, req_id: str) -> Optional[int]:
        """This method must always be followed by a call to condense().
        
        Args:
          req_id: request to remove

        Returns:
          Removed request index, or `None` if `req_id` not recognized
        """

        req_index = self.req_id_to_index.pop(req_id, None)
        if req_index is None:
            return None
        self.batch_update_builder.removed_append(req_index)
        self._req_ids[req_index] = None
        self.req_output_token_ids[req_index] = None

        self.greedy_reqs.discard(req_id)
        self.random_reqs.discard(req_id)
        self.top_p_reqs.discard(req_id)
        self.top_k_reqs.discard(req_id)
        self.spec_decode_unsupported_reqs.discard(req_id)
        self.frequency_penalties_reqs.discard(req_id)
        self.presence_penalties_reqs.discard(req_id)
        self.repetition_penalties_reqs.discard(req_id)
        self.generators.pop(req_index, None)
        self.num_logprobs.pop(req_id, None)
        self.num_prompt_logprobs.pop(req_id, None)
        self.in_progress_prompt_logprobs_cpu.pop(req_id, None)

        # LoRA
        lora_id = self.request_lora_mapping[req_index]
        if lora_id != 0:
            self.lora_id_to_request_ids[lora_id].discard(req_id)
            if len(self.lora_id_to_request_ids[lora_id]) == 0:
                self.lora_id_to_request_ids.pop(lora_id)
                self.lora_id_to_lora_request.pop(lora_id)
            self.request_lora_mapping[req_index] = 0

        self.has_allowed_token_ids.discard(req_id)
        if self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor is not None:
            # False means we don't fill with -inf.
            self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[req_index].fill_(False)
        self.bad_words_token_ids.pop(req_index, None)
        self.pooling_params.pop(req_id, None)
        return req_index

    def swap_states(self, i1: int, i2: int) -> None:
        self.batch_update_builder.moved.append(
            (i1, i2, MoveDirectionality.SWAP))
        old_id_i1 = self._req_ids[i1]
        old_id_i2 = self._req_ids[i2]
        self._req_ids[i1], self._req_ids[i2] =\
            self._req_ids[i2], self._req_ids[i1] # noqa
        self.req_output_token_ids[i1], self.req_output_token_ids[i2] =\
            self.req_output_token_ids[i2], self.req_output_token_ids[i1]
        assert old_id_i1 is not None and old_id_i2 is not None
        self.req_id_to_index[old_id_i1], self.req_id_to_index[old_id_i2] =\
            self.req_id_to_index[old_id_i2], self.req_id_to_index[old_id_i1]
        self.num_tokens[i1], self.num_tokens[i2] =\
            self.num_tokens[i2], self.num_tokens[i1]
        self.num_tokens_no_spec[i1], self.num_tokens_no_spec[i2] =\
            self.num_tokens_no_spec[i2], self.num_tokens_no_spec[i1]
        self.num_prompt_tokens[i1], self.num_prompt_tokens[i2] =\
            self.num_prompt_tokens[i2], self.num_prompt_tokens[i1]
        self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[i1], self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[i2] =\
            self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[i2], self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[i1]
        self.temperature_cpu[i1], self.temperature_cpu[i2] =\
            self.temperature_cpu[i2], self.temperature_cpu[i1]
        self.top_p_cpu[i1], self.top_p_cpu[i2] =\
            self.top_p_cpu[i2], self.top_p_cpu[i1]
        self.top_k_cpu[i1], self.top_k_cpu[i2] =\
            self.top_k_cpu[i2], self.top_k_cpu[i1]
        self.frequency_penalties_cpu[i1], self.frequency_penalties_cpu[i2] =\
            self.frequency_penalties_cpu[i2], self.frequency_penalties_cpu[i1]
        self.presence_penalties_cpu[i1], self.presence_penalties_cpu[i2] =\
            self.presence_penalties_cpu[i2], self.presence_penalties_cpu[i1]
        self.repetition_penalties_cpu[i1], self.repetition_penalties_cpu[i2] =\
            self.repetition_penalties_cpu[i2], self.repetition_penalties_cpu[i1]

        # NOTE: the following is unsafe
        # self.token_ids_cpu[i1, ...], self.token_ids_cpu[i2, ...], =\
        #     self.token_ids_cpu[i2, ...], self.token_ids_cpu[i1, ...]
        # instead, we need to temporiarily copy the data for one of the indices
        # TODO(lucas): optimize this by only copying valid indices
        tmp = self.token_ids_cpu[i1, ...].copy()
        self.token_ids_cpu[i1, ...] = self.token_ids_cpu[i2, ...]
        self.token_ids_cpu[i2, ...] = tmp

        swap_dict_values(self.generators, i1, i2)
        swap_dict_values(self.bad_words_token_ids, i1, i2)

        self.request_lora_mapping[i1], self.request_lora_mapping[i2] =\
            self.request_lora_mapping[i2], self.request_lora_mapping[i1]

        if self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor is not None:
            self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[i1], \
                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[i2] =\
                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[i2], \
                    self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[i1]
        self.block_table.swap_row(i1, i2)

    def condense(self) -> None:
        """Slide non-empty requests down into lower, empty indices.

        Any consecutive empty indices at the very end of the list are not
        filled.

        Args:
          empty_req_indices: empty indices which may be filled.

        Returns:
          swaps: list of (from,to) swap tuples for moved requests
          empty_req_indices: indices not filled by condensation
        """
        if not (empty_req_indices := self.batch_update_builder.removed):
            # All removed requests were replaced by added requests, or else no
            # requests were removed at all. No condense() needed
            return
        num_reqs = self.num_reqs
        if num_reqs == 0:
            # The batched states are empty.
            self._req_ids.clear()
            self.req_output_token_ids.clear()
            return

        # NOTE(woosuk): This function assumes that the empty_req_indices
        # is sorted in descending order.
        last_req_index = num_reqs + len(empty_req_indices) - 1
        while empty_req_indices:
            # Find the largest non-empty index.
            while last_req_index in empty_req_indices:
                last_req_index -= 1

            # Find the smallest empty index.
            empty_index = self.batch_update_builder.peek_removed()
            assert empty_index is not None
            if empty_index >= last_req_index:
                break

            # Move active request down into empty request
            # index.
            self.batch_update_builder.pop_removed()
            self.batch_update_builder.moved.append(
                (last_req_index, empty_index,
                 MoveDirectionality.UNIDIRECTIONAL))
            req_id = self._req_ids[last_req_index]
            output_token_ids = self.req_output_token_ids[last_req_index]
            assert req_id is not None
            self._req_ids[empty_index] = req_id
            self._req_ids[last_req_index] = None
            self.req_output_token_ids[empty_index] = output_token_ids
            self.req_output_token_ids[last_req_index] = None
            self.req_id_to_index[req_id] = empty_index

            num_tokens = self.num_tokens[last_req_index]
            self.token_ids_cpu[empty_index, :num_tokens] = self.token_ids_cpu[
                last_req_index, :num_tokens]
            self.num_tokens[empty_index] = num_tokens
            self.num_tokens_no_spec[empty_index] = self.num_tokens_no_spec[
                last_req_index]
            self.num_prompt_tokens[empty_index] = self.num_prompt_tokens[
                last_req_index]
            self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[
                empty_index] = self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[last_req_index]
            self.block_table.move_row(last_req_index, empty_index)
            self.temperature_cpu[empty_index] = self.temperature_cpu[
                last_req_index]
            self.top_p_cpu[empty_index] = self.top_p_cpu[last_req_index]
            self.top_k_cpu[empty_index] = self.top_k_cpu[last_req_index]
            self.frequency_penalties_cpu[
                empty_index] = self.frequency_penalties_cpu[last_req_index]
            self.presence_penalties_cpu[
                empty_index] = self.presence_penalties_cpu[last_req_index]
            self.repetition_penalties_cpu[
                empty_index] = self.repetition_penalties_cpu[last_req_index]
            generator = self.generators.pop(last_req_index, None)
            if generator is not None:
                self.generators[empty_index] = generator

            self.request_lora_mapping[empty_index] = self.request_lora_mapping[
                last_req_index]

            # TODO convert these to LogitsProcessors
            if self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor is not None:
                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[
                    empty_index] = self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[
                        last_req_index]

            bad_words_token_ids = self.bad_words_token_ids.pop(
                last_req_index, None)
            if bad_words_token_ids is not None:
                self.bad_words_token_ids[empty_index] = bad_words_token_ids

            # Decrement last_req_index since it is now empty.
            last_req_index -= 1

        # Trim lists to the batch size.
        del self._req_ids[self.num_reqs:]
        del self.req_output_token_ids[self.num_reqs:]

    def refresh_metadata(self):
        """Apply batch updates, reset input batch at end of step
        
        * Apply batch add/remove/permute to logits procs' states
        * If batch state is modified, update sampling metadata
        """
        batch_update = self.batch_update_builder.get_and_reset(self.num_reqs)
        for logit_proc in self.logitsprocs.all:
            logit_proc.update_state(batch_update)
        if batch_update:
            self.sampling_metadata = self._make_sampling_metadata()

    def _make_sampling_metadata(self) -> SamplingMetadata:
        num_reqs = self.num_reqs
        if not self.all_greedy:
            temperature = copy_slice(self.temperature_cpu_tensor,
                                     self.temperature, num_reqs)
        else:
            temperature = None
        if not self.no_top_p:
            copy_slice(self.top_p_cpu_tensor, self.top_p, num_reqs)
        if not self.no_top_k:
            copy_slice(self.top_k_cpu_tensor, self.top_k, num_reqs)

        if not self.no_penalties:
            # Since syncing these tensors is expensive only copy them
            # if necessary i.e. if there are requests which require
            # penalties to be applied during sampling.
            copy_slice(self.frequency_penalties_cpu_tensor,
                       self.frequency_penalties, num_reqs)
            copy_slice(self.presence_penalties_cpu_tensor,
                       self.presence_penalties, num_reqs)
            copy_slice(self.repetition_penalties_cpu_tensor,
                       self.repetition_penalties, num_reqs)

        needs_prompt_token_ids = (not self.no_penalties or
                                  (self.num_reqs > 0
                                   and self.logits_processing_needs_token_ids))
        if needs_prompt_token_ids:
            # The prompt tokens are used only for applying penalties or
            # step pooling during the sampling/pooling process.
            # Hence copy these tensors only when there are requests which
            # need penalties/step_pooler to be applied.
            prompt_token_ids = self._make_prompt_token_ids_tensor()
        else:
            prompt_token_ids = None

        allowed_token_ids_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
        if not self.no_allowed_token_ids:
            assert self.allowed_token_ids_mask is not None
            copy_slice(self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor,
                       self.allowed_token_ids_mask, num_reqs)
            allowed_token_ids_mask = self.allowed_token_ids_mask[:num_reqs]

        return SamplingMetadata(
            temperature=temperature,
            all_greedy=self.all_greedy,
            all_random=self.all_random,
            top_p=None if self.no_top_p else self.top_p[:num_reqs],
            top_k=None if self.no_top_k else self.top_k[:num_reqs],
            generators=self.generators,
            max_num_logprobs=self.max_num_logprobs,
            prompt_token_ids=prompt_token_ids,
            frequency_penalties=self.frequency_penalties[:num_reqs],
            presence_penalties=self.presence_penalties[:num_reqs],
            repetition_penalties=self.repetition_penalties[:num_reqs],
            output_token_ids=cast(list[list[int]], self.req_output_token_ids),
            no_penalties=self.no_penalties,
            allowed_token_ids_mask=allowed_token_ids_mask,
            bad_words_token_ids=self.bad_words_token_ids,
            logitsprocs=self.logitsprocs,
        )

    @property
    def pooling_metadata(self) -> PoolingMetadata:
        if len(self.pooling_params) == 0:
            pooling_params = []
        else:
            # Note, for now this assumes that all request in the batch
            # are either sampling or pooling requests
            assert len(self.req_ids) == len(self.pooling_params)
            pooling_params = [
                self.pooling_params[req_id] for req_id in self.req_ids
            ]

        return PoolingMetadata(
            prompt_lens=torch.from_numpy(
                self.num_prompt_tokens[:self.num_reqs]).to(self.device),
            prompt_token_ids=self.sampling_metadata.prompt_token_ids,
            pooling_params=pooling_params,
        )

    def _make_prompt_token_ids_tensor(self) -> torch.Tensor:
        max_prompt_len = self.num_prompt_tokens[:self.num_reqs].max()
        prompt_token_ids_cpu_tensor = torch.empty(
            (self.num_reqs, max_prompt_len),
            device="cpu",
            dtype=torch.int64,
            pin_memory=self.pin_memory,
        )
        prompt_token_ids = prompt_token_ids_cpu_tensor.numpy()
        prompt_token_ids[:] = self.token_ids_cpu[:self.
                                                 num_reqs, :max_prompt_len]
        # Use the value of vocab_size as a pad since we don't have a
        # token_id of this value.
        for i in range(self.num_reqs):
            prompt_token_ids[i, self.num_prompt_tokens[i]:] = self.vocab_size
        return prompt_token_ids_cpu_tensor.to(device=self.device,
                                              non_blocking=True)

    def make_lora_inputs(
        self, num_scheduled_tokens: np.ndarray
    ) -> tuple[tuple[int, ...], tuple[int, ...], set[LoRARequest]]:
        """
        Given the num_scheduled_tokens for each request in the batch, return
        datastructures used to activate the current LoRAs.
        Returns:
            1. prompt_lora_mapping: A tuple of size self.num_reqs where,
               prompt_lora_mapping[i] is the LoRA id to use for the ith prompt.
            2. token_lora_mapping: A tuple of size np.sum(num_scheduled_tokens)
               where, token_lora_mapping[i] is the LoRA id to use for ith token.
            3. lora_requests: Set of relevant LoRA requests.
        """

        req_lora_mapping = self.request_lora_mapping[:self.num_reqs]
        prompt_lora_mapping = tuple(req_lora_mapping)
        token_lora_mapping = tuple(
            req_lora_mapping.repeat(num_scheduled_tokens))
        active_lora_requests: set[LoRARequest] = set(
            self.lora_id_to_lora_request.values())

        return prompt_lora_mapping, token_lora_mapping, active_lora_requests

    @property
    def num_reqs(self) -> int:
        return len(self.req_id_to_index)

    @property
    def all_greedy(self) -> bool:
        return len(self.random_reqs) == 0

    @property
    def all_random(self) -> bool:
        return len(self.greedy_reqs) == 0

    @property
    def no_top_p(self) -> bool:
        return len(self.top_p_reqs) == 0

    @property
    def no_top_k(self) -> bool:
        return len(self.top_k_reqs) == 0

    @property
    def no_penalties(self) -> bool:
        return (len(self.presence_penalties_reqs) == 0
                and len(self.frequency_penalties_reqs) == 0
                and len(self.repetition_penalties_reqs) == 0)

    @property
    def max_num_logprobs(self) -> Optional[int]:
        return max(self.num_logprobs.values()) if self.num_logprobs else None

    @property
    def no_prompt_logprob(self) -> bool:
        return not self.num_prompt_logprobs

    @property
    def no_allowed_token_ids(self) -> bool:
        return len(self.has_allowed_token_ids) == 0

content.content:
1	# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
2	# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright contributors to the vLLM project
3	"""A layer that samples the next tokens from the model's outputs."""
4	
5	import torch
6	import torch.nn as nn
7	
8	from vllm.utils import is_pin_memory_available
9	from vllm.v1.outputs import LogprobsTensors, SamplerOutput
10	from vllm.v1.sample.metadata import SamplingMetadata
11	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.bad_words import apply_bad_words
12	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.penalties import apply_all_penalties
13	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.topk_topp_sampler import TopKTopPSampler
14	
15	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5
16	
17	
18	class Sampler(nn.Module):
19	
20	    def __init__(self):
21	        super().__init__()
22	        self.topk_topp_sampler = TopKTopPSampler()
23	        self.pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()
24	
25	    def forward(
26	        self,
27	        logits: torch.Tensor,
28	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
29	    ) -> SamplerOutput:
30	        # NOTE(woosuk): Use the original logits (before any penalties or
31	        # temperature scaling) for the top-k logprobs.
32	        # This is different from the V0 sampler, which uses the logits that
33	        # is used for sampling (after penalties and temperature scaling).
34	        # TODO(rob): provide option for logprobs post sampling.
35	        # See https://vllm-dev.slack.com/archives/C07UUL8E61Z/p1735907856007919 # noqa: E501
36	        num_logprobs = sampling_metadata.max_num_logprobs
37	        if num_logprobs is not None:
38	            raw_logprobs = self.compute_logprobs(logits)
39	
40	        # Use float32 for the logits.
41	        logits = logits.to(torch.float32)
42	        # Apply allowed token ids.
43	        logits = self.apply_allowed_token_ids(logits, sampling_metadata)
44	        # Apply bad words exclusion.
45	        logits = self.apply_bad_words(logits, sampling_metadata)
46	
47	        # Apply logits processors which can impact greedy sampling
48	        for processor in (sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.non_argmax_invariant):
49	            logits = processor.apply(logits)
50	
51	        # Apply penalties (e.g., min_tokens, freq_penalties).
52	        logits = self.apply_penalties(logits, sampling_metadata)
53	        # Sample the next token.
54	        sampled = self.sample(logits, sampling_metadata)
55	        # Convert sampled token ids to int64 (long) type to ensure compatibility
56	        # with subsequent operations that may use these values as indices.
57	        # This conversion is necessary because FlashInfer sampling operations
58	        # return int32 (while PyTorch argmax and topk return int64).
59	        sampled = sampled.long()
60	
61	        # Gather the logprobs of the topk and sampled token (if requested).
62	        # Get logprobs and rank tensors (if requested)
63	        logprobs_tensors = None if num_logprobs is None else \
64	            self.gather_logprobs(raw_logprobs, num_logprobs, token_ids=sampled)
65	
66	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
67	        sampled = sampled.to(torch.int32)
68	
69	        # These are GPU tensors.
70	        sampler_output = SamplerOutput(
71	            # The sampled tokens are expanded to 2D tensor with shape
72	            # [num_requests, 1], where each row represents one generated
73	            # token per request.
74	            sampled_token_ids=sampled.unsqueeze(-1),
75	            logprobs_tensors=logprobs_tensors,
76	        )
77	        return sampler_output
78	
79	    def apply_temperature(
80	        self,
81	        logits: torch.Tensor,
82	        temp: torch.Tensor,
83	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
84	        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
85	        return logits.div_(temp.unsqueeze(dim=1))
86	
87	    def greedy_sample(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
88	        return logits.argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
89	
90	    def sample(
91	        self,
92	        logits: torch.Tensor,
93	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
94	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
95	        """Sample logits based on sampling metadata.
96	
97	        The various logits processing functions called in this method
98	        may update the logits tensor in-place.
99	        """
100	
101	        assert not (sampling_metadata.all_greedy
102	                    and sampling_metadata.all_random)
103	        if sampling_metadata.all_random:
104	            greedy_sampled = None
105	        else:
106	            greedy_sampled = self.greedy_sample(logits)
107	            if sampling_metadata.all_greedy:
108	                return greedy_sampled
109	
110	        assert sampling_metadata.temperature is not None
111	
112	        # Apply temperature.
113	        logits = self.apply_temperature(logits, sampling_metadata.temperature)
114	
115	        # Apply logits processors that only apply to random sampling
116	        # (argmax invariant)
117	        for processor in sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.argmax_invariant:
118	            logits = processor.apply(logits)
119	
120	        # Apply top_k and/or top_p.
121	        random_sampled = self.topk_topp_sampler(
122	            logits,
123	            sampling_metadata.generators,
124	            sampling_metadata.top_k,
125	            sampling_metadata.top_p,
126	        )
127	
128	        if greedy_sampled is None:
129	            return random_sampled
130	
131	        sampled = torch.where(
132	            sampling_metadata.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS,
133	            greedy_sampled,
134	            random_sampled,
135	            out=greedy_sampled,  # Reuse tensor
136	        )
137	        return sampled
138	
139	    def compute_logprobs(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
140	        return logits.log_softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
141	
142	    def gather_logprobs(
143	        self,
144	        logprobs: torch.Tensor,
145	        num_logprobs: int,
146	        token_ids: torch.Tensor,
147	    ) -> LogprobsTensors:
148	        """
149	        Gather logprobs for topk and sampled/prompt token.
150	
151	        Args:
152	          logprobs: (num tokens) x (vocab) tensor
153	          num_logprobs: minimum number of logprobs to
154	                        retain per token
155	          token_ids: prompt tokens (if prompt logprobs)
156	                     or sampled tokens (if sampled
157	                     logprobs); 1D token ID tensor
158	                     with (num tokens) elements
159	                     Must be int64.

161	        Returns:
162	          Top-k int indices tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
163	          Top-k float logprobs tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
164	          Sampled token rank tensor, (num tokens)
165	        """
166	        assert token_ids.dtype == torch.int64
167	        # Find the topK values.
168	        topk_logprobs, topk_indices = torch.topk(logprobs,
169	                                                 num_logprobs,
170	                                                 dim=-1)
171	
172	        # Get with the logprob of the prompt or sampled token.
173	        token_ids = token_ids.unsqueeze(-1)
174	        token_logprobs = logprobs.gather(-1, token_ids)
175	
176	        # Compute the ranks of the actual token.
177	        token_ranks = (logprobs >= token_logprobs).sum(-1)
178	
179	        # Concatenate together with the topk.
180	        indices = torch.cat((token_ids, topk_indices), dim=1)
181	        logprobs = torch.cat((token_logprobs, topk_logprobs), dim=1)
182	
183	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.

194	            assert sampling_metadata.prompt_token_ids is not None

196	                logits,
